subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Identify the five basic steps of nutrition in animals
Recognize and label the main organs of the human digestive system
Understand the difference between breathing and cellular respiration in simple terms
Describe how humans and other animals (fish, insects, earthworms) breathe
Identify the components of the circulatory system (Heart and Blood)
Understand the basic role of kidneys in removing liquid waste
Relate healthy habits to the proper functioning of body systems

lesson_plan.title: Nutrition: How Food is Used
topics_covered: The 5 steps: Ingestion, Digestion, Absorption, Assimilation, Egestion
Different ways of taking in food (Scraping, Siphoning, Chewing)
Digestion in Amoeba (Simple view of pseudopodia)
teaching_strategy: Picture sorting: Match animals to their feeding methods
student_activities: Identify feeding modes of common animals (Bee, Snail, Eagle)
Draw a 'Nutrition Ladder' with the 5 steps

lesson_plan.title: The Human Digestive System
topics_covered: Organs: Mouth, Oesophagus, Stomach, Intestines
The role of teeth (Types and care)
Importance of saliva and water in digestion
teaching_strategy: Using a large colorful human torso chart
Comparison: Mouth as a 'grinder' and stomach as a 'mixer'
student_activities: Label a 'Fill-in-the-Organs' worksheet
Use a mirror to count and identify their own teeth
assessment: Quick-fire round: Name the organ's job

lesson_plan.title: Breathing: In and Out
topics_covered: The mechanism of breathing (Inhalation vs. Exhalation)
Organs involved: Nose, Windpipe, Lungs, Diaphragm
Breathing rate and physical activity
teaching_strategy: Class discussion on why we pant after running
student_activities: Record breaths per minute while sitting vs. after spot-jogging
Observe the movement of the balloon in the lung model
assessment: Exit slip: Name two things that happen during inhalation

lesson_plan.title: Breathing in the Animal World
topics_covered: Gills in Fish (Breathing underwater)
Spiracles in Cockroaches
Skin breathing in Earthworms/Frogs
teaching_strategy: Visual aids/Videos of fish and insects
Comparison table: Lungs vs. Gills vs. Spiracles
Analogy: Spiracles as 'tiny windows' for air
student_activities: Draw a fish and highlight the gill area
Match animals to their breathing methods in a fun puzzle
assessment: Short answer: 'How does an earthworm stay moist?'

lesson_plan.title: The Heart and Blood
topics_covered: The Heart as a non-stop pump
Blood components: Red (Oxygen) and White (Fighting germs)
Pulse rate: Feeling the heartbeat in the wrist
teaching_strategy: Simple chart showing blood's 'delivery service' job
student_activities: Learn to find and count their pulse rate
Listen to a 'Heartbeat beatbox' to understand the rhythm (Lub-Dub)
assessment: Oral quiz: What is the main job of the heart?

lesson_plan.title: Excretion: Removing Waste
topics_covered: Why we need to remove waste (Toxins)
The Kidneys and Urinary Bladder
Sweat as a way to cool down and clean the body
teaching_strategy: Simple diagram of the human excretory system
Discussion on the importance of drinking water
student_activities: Trace and color the urinary system diagram
Brainstorm: 'What are 3 ways waste leaves our body?'

lesson_plan.title: Revision: The Healthy Body
topics_covered: Recap of all systems working together
Healthy food for a healthy heart and stomach
Doubt clearing session
student_activities: Participate in the 'Life Processes' quiz
Complete the chapter summary worksheet
assessment: Review of the final body-system mind map

teaching_aids: Sieve and colored water for filtration demo
teacher_tip: Keep the focus on 'Function over Formula'. For an easy level, it's more important that they know *where* the heart is and *what* it does rather than the names of every valve. Use the 'Delivery Service' analogy for blood—it picks up oxygen and drops off waste!
